hint: The 'H' refers to links (hyperlinks) and the 'M' is for markup — structure of web pages
explanation:
HTML (HyperText Markup Language) is the standard language for web pages. 'HyperText' = text with links. 'Markup' = using tags like

,

to structure content. Created by Tim Berners-Lee in 1991.

q: What is the binary representation of the decimal number 13?
explanation: 13 in binary: 13÷2=6 R1, 6÷2=3 R0, 3÷2=1 R1, 1÷2=0 R1. Reading remainders bottom-up: 1101. Verify: 1×8 + 1×4 + 0×2 + 1×1 = 13 ✓

q: Which data structure works on the LIFO (Last In, First Out) principle?
hint: Think of a stack of plates — you always add and remove from the top
explanation: A Stack follows LIFO: the last element added (pushed) is the first one removed (popped). Real-world examples: browser back button, undo in text editors, function call management in programs.

q: Who is known as the 'Father of the Internet'?
hint: He co-created the TCP/IP protocols that power the internet
explanation: Vint Cerf (with Bob Kahn) designed TCP/IP in 1974 — the fundamental protocols of the internet. Tim Berners-Lee invented the World Wide Web (WWW) in 1989, which runs ON TOP of the internet.

q: What is a 'deadlock' in operating systems?
o: When two processes wait for each other indefinitely
a: When two processes wait for each other indefinitely
hint: Imagine two people each holding one key the other needs, both waiting
explanation: Deadlock occurs when 2+ processes each hold a resource and wait for the other's resource — creating a circular dependency. Four conditions for deadlock: Mutual Exclusion, Hold & Wait, No Preemption, Circular Wait.

hint: It's used to manage and manipulate relational databases
explanation: SQL (Structured Query Language) is used to interact with relational databases. Core commands: SELECT (retrieve), INSERT (add), UPDATE (modify), DELETE (remove), CREATE (build tables). Invented by IBM in the 1970s.

q: In Object-Oriented Programming, what does 'encapsulation' mean?
o: Wrapping data and methods in a single unit
a: Wrapping data and methods in a single unit
hint: Think of a capsule/pill that contains everything inside it
explanation: Encapsulation bundles data (attributes) and methods (functions) together inside a class, controlling access via public/private/protected modifiers. It protects internal state and reduces dependencies. Combined with abstraction, it's a core OOP pillar.

q: What is the time complexity of binary search?
hint: Each step eliminates HALF of the remaining elements
explanation: Binary search on a sorted array takes O(log n) because each comparison halves the search space. With 1 million elements, it takes at most log₂(1,000,000) ≈ 20 comparisons! Linear search would take up to 1,000,000.
